adds.rationale: NuScale filed its 77 MWe VOYGR-6 Standard Design Approval application with the NRC on January 1, 2023 — a concrete regulatory milestone signaling the nuclear SMR buildup phase is advancing — and the watchlist has zero nuclear coverage against the profile's explicit SMR next-wave declaration.

news_evidence.summary: NuScale Power Corporation completed submission of a Standard Design Approval application to the NRC on January 1, 2023 for its uprated 250 MWt (77 MWe) VOYGR-6 six-module SMR design, built on the same fundamental passive-safety case that earned NuScale the first-ever NRC approval of any SMR design.

NuScale management scheduled one-on-one investor meetings at the NYSE Energy & Utilities Virtual Investor Access Day (March 23), UBS Infrastructure Conference Dallas (March 23), and Wells Fargo Clean Energy Symposium New York (March 28), indicating active institutional outreach around its SMR commercialization program during Q1 2023.

NuScale's original 50 MWe SMR design became the first in history to receive NRC Standard Design Approval, establishing NuScale as the only SMR developer with a certified US design ahead of the 2023 VOYGR-6 uprate application.

removes.rationale: SPY is a broad-market index ETF with no exposure to any of the profile's declared waves; the watchlist now has four AI names plus three specific next-wave plays, making a general index slot redundant and better deployed toward the nuclear gap via SMR.

news_evidence.summary: Silicon Valley Bank collapsed March 10, 2023 (second-largest US bank failure in history); US Treasury designated it a systemic risk and activated emergency depositor backstops; the KBW Nasdaq Bank Index fell 7.7% while the S&P 500 fell 1.7% on the same day — broad-market index exposure concentrates macro-systemic risk while adding no thesis-specific upside.

rationale_overall: The single swap this quarter removes SPY (a general-market index ETF occupying a thesis-free slot) and adds SMR (NuScale Power, nuclear bucket), closing the most glaring next-wave gap at a moment when NuScale's January 1, 2023 NRC Standard Design Approval submission for its uprated 77 MWe VOYGR-6 module marks a concrete regulatory buildup signal the profile explicitly called out. Post-swap the watchlist carries four AI names (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA), one rockets/spacecraft name (RKLB), one robotics ETF (BOTZ), one defense/geopolitical ETF (ITA), and one nuclear name (SMR), reaching the eight-ticker cap with no bucket exceeding four tickers. Quantum computing (IONQ is the leading candidate) and aging-demographics remain uncovered but must wait for a future remove to free a slot; they are the top priority for the next rebalance.
